# standdown

> Client-side, advisory affiliate stand-down for browser extensions and shopping
> tools. `standdown` decides whether your extension should stay quiet because a
> partner already owns the current affiliate attribution — so you don't hijack a
> sale someone else referred. It returns a `Decision` and fires nothing itself;
> you own the side effects. Decisions are local and synchronous, signals are a
> closed type that excludes user identity, and it fails toward standing down.

Install: `npm install standdown`. Published ESM + CJS. Four entry points:
`standdown` (core), `standdown/policies` (network packs), `standdown/webext`
(Chromium MV3 adapter), `standdown/content` (Safari / page-level adapter).

Minimal MV3 integration:

```ts
import { allPolicies } from 'standdown/policies';
import { createStanddown } from 'standdown/webext';
const standdown = createStanddown({ policies: allPolicies, publisherSites: ['your-site.com'] });
// content/popup: const { decision } = await chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ type: 'standdown:shouldStandDown', tabId, url });
// if (decision.standDown) return;  // suppress activation
```

## Integration

- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md): step-by-step playbook for an AI agent adding standdown to an extension (install → adapter → integrate → build/bundle → grade).
- [Webext quickstart](./README.md): `createStanddown()` for Chromium MV3; requires `chrome.webNavigation.onCommitted`.
- [Content quickstart](./README.md): `createContentStanddown()` for Safari / reduced-permission; `await standdown.ready`; handle `decision.degraded`.
- [examples/mv3-extension](./examples/mv3-extension): working MV3 background worker + popup, with the required esbuild bundle step (subpath imports don't resolve raw in extension contexts).

## Reference

- [README.md](./README.md): full API, `selfPatterns` self-exemption scope, per-host disable, degraded decisions, interop.
- [POLICIES.md](./POLICIES.md): bundled network packs (cj, impact, rakuten, awin, shareasale, ebay-epn, amazon, universal) with citations. `allPolicies` is the verified set; `experimentalPolicies` (skimlinks, partnerize) are opt-in.
- [audit/](./audit): conformance grader — loads a real unpacked extension in a real browser and scores stand-down F→A+, with an inert cap so dead code can't score well.

## Invariants

- I1 client-side decisions only · I2 no user profiling (closed signal type) · I3 fail toward standing down · I4 monotone remote updates · I5 audit log on by default.
